Title: Identification of BAHD-acyltransferase enzymes involved in ingenane diterpenoid biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Euphorbia peplus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Euphorbia peplus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana; VIGS in E. peplus via pTRV2-EpCH42 construct
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Primarily expressed in stem tissue; low in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In E. peplus stems transcript levels significantly reduced by VIGS; low in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase family; Clade 3 plant specialized metabolism acyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late acylation step of ingenol branch
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed late step; direct product-forming acylation step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an angelyl group from angelyl-CoA to ingenol at the C3 position to form ingenol-3-angelate
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potent antitumor activity; reported anticancer, anti-HIV, and anti-inflammatory activities for related Euphorbiaceae diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Predominantly latex; traces in all tested tissues
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Angelic acid feeding required in N. benthamiana assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient pathway reconstitution in Nicotiana benthamiana; in vitro assay with recombinant protein from E. coli; VIGS in Euphorbia peplus; LC-MS metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ingenol-3-angelate accumulation and downstream ingenol-3-angelate-20-acetate production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EpBAHD-08 catalyzes C3 angeloylation of ingenol to form ingenol-3-angelate in N. benthamiana and in vitro. 2. VIGS significantly reduced ingenol-3-angelate and ingenol-3-angelate-20-acetate levels and increased ingenol in E. peplus stems, demonstrating in planta necessity. 3. The gene was designated ingenol-3-angelate synthase (I3AS).
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a committed and essential control step for ingenol-3-angelate formation in planta
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to increase ingenol-3-angelate production in planta by overexpression; conversely silencing/knockout can redirect accumulation toward nonacylated ingenol derivatives for semisynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing reduces desired ingenol-3-angelate and downstream acetate derivative while increasing precursor ingenol
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: EpCCL2 used in heterologous reconstruction but not required in N. benthamiana because of endogenous activity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In vitro with angelyl-CoA produced ingenol-3-angelate as minor product and ingenol-3-tiglate as major product, suggesting acyl-donor isomerization can occur before transfer

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Euphorbia peplus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Euphorbia peplus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana; VIGS in E. peplus via pTRV2-EpCH42 construct
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Primarily expressed in stem tissue; low in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In E. peplus stems transcript levels significantly reduced by VIGS; low in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase family; Clade 3 plant specialized metabolism acyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late acylation step with side activity toward ingenol branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Can transfer angelyl or tiglyl moieties to ingenol in heterologous and in vitro assays, forming ingenol-3-angelate and ingenol-3-tiglate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Can directly form target product in assay but likely side activity relative to native role
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potent antitumor activity; reported anticancer, anti-HIV, and anti-inflammatory activities for related Euphorbiaceae diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Angelic acid feeding required in N. benthamiana assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; in vitro recombinant enzyme assay; VIGS in Euphorbia peplus; LC-MS metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Shows low angeloylation activity toward ingenol in assays, but silencing mainly alters jatrophane diterpenoid profile rather than ingenane levels in planta
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EpBAHD-06 converts ingenol to ingenol-3-angelate in N. benthamiana and in vitro, but activity is lower than EpBAHD-08. 2. VIGS did not significantly affect ingenane levels, indicating it is not essential for ingenol-3-angelate formation in planta. 3. Silencing shifted jatrophane profiles, supporting a native role in jatrophane metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established for ingenol-3-angelate; likely not key control step of ingenane branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: More relevant for engineering jatrophane acylation patterns than for boosting ingenol-3-angelate
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: EpCCL2 used in heterologous reconstruction but not required in N. benthamiana because of endogenous activity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors infer ingenol acylation may be side activity; VIGS increased Jatrophanes 1 and 7 and decreased Jatrophanes 2-5

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Euphorbia peplus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Euphorbia peplus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana; VIGS in E. peplus via pTRV2-EpCH42 construct
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Primarily expressed in stem tissue; low in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Modest reduction under VIGS in stem tissue
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase family; Clade 3 plant specialized metabolism acyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late acetylation step downstream of ingenol-3-angelate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acetyl group from acetyl-CoA to ingenol-3-angelate at C20 to produce ingenol-3-angelate-20-acetate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms downstream derivative of target metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Biologically active diterpenoid derivative from E. peplus
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Predominantly latex; traces in all tested tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with EpBAHD-08; in vitro recombinant enzyme assay; VIGS; LC-MS; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes acetylation of ingenol-3-angelate to ingenol-3-angelate-20-acetate in assay systems, but silencing alone or with EpBAHD-11 did not alter metabolite levels in planta due to redundancy
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EpBAHD-07 acetylates ingenol-3-angelate to ingenol-3-angelate-20-acetate in N. benthamiana and in vitro. 2. VIGS failed to produce a clear metabolite phenotype, indicating redundancy. 3. Additional homologs such as EpBAHD-12 likely contribute to the same step.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Silencing or knockout together with redundant homologs may enhance ingenol-3-angelate accumulation by limiting conversion to acetate derivative
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single-gene manipulation may show little effect because of functional redundancy
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: EpBAHD-08 used upstream in transient assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Close homolog EpBAHD-12 also catalyzes same acetylation step

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Euphorbia peplus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Euphorbia peplus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana; VIGS in E. peplus via pTRV2-EpCH42 construct
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Primarily expressed in stem tissue; low in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Modest but statistically significant reduction under VIGS in stem tissue
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase family; Clade 3 plant specialized metabolism acyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late acetylation step downstream of ingenol-3-angelate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acetyl group from acetyl-CoA to ingenol-3-angelate at C20 to produce ingenol-3-angelate-20-acetate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms downstream derivative of target metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Biologically active diterpenoid derivative from E. peplus
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Predominantly latex; traces in all tested tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with EpBAHD-08; in vitro recombinant enzyme assay; VIGS; LC-MS; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes acetylation of ingenol-3-angelate to ingenol-3-angelate-20-acetate in assay systems, but no decisive in planta phenotype due to redundancy
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EpBAHD-11 acetylates ingenol-3-angelate to ingenol-3-angelate-20-acetate in N. benthamiana and in vitro. 2. Silencing alone or together with EpBAHD-07 did not significantly alter ingenane accumulation in planta. 3. Results support substantial redundancy at this enzymatic step.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May need multiplex knockout with EpBAHD-07/EpBAHD-12 and other homologs to increase ingenol-3-angelate accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Redundancy complicates trait engineering and phenotypic detection
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: EpBAHD-08 used upstream in transient assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Slight jatrophane changes upon single-gene silencing were not reproduced in double-silencing experiment

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript levels an order of magnitude lower than EpBAHD-07
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase family; close homologue of EpBAHD-07
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late acetylation step downstream of ingenol-3-angelate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes acetylation of ingenol-3-angelate at C20 to form ingenol-3-angelate-20-acetate in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms downstream derivative of target metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Biologically active diterpenoid derivative from E. peplus
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq candidate identification; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Additional redundant acetyltransferase capable of converting ingenol-3-angelate to ingenol-3-angelate-20-acetate
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EpBAHD-12 is a close homolog of EpBAHD-07 identified from RNA-seq inspection. 2. It catalyzes C20 acetylation of ingenol-3-angelate in N. benthamiana. 3. Its lower expression but shared activity supports redundancy in the acetate-forming step.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Should be included in multiplex editing strategies targeting redundant acetate-forming enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower expression may limit contribution, but redundancy can buffer engineering effects
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: EpBAHD-08 used upstream in transient assay context
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not functionally tested by VIGS in this study
